reviews.review_description: Stay away brother, if you're contemplating buying this game to experience 40k universe and lore forget it. I didn't spend much time in game like first thing and it smells like dei when you add poor graphics terrible physics and lack of options in settings its awful

The graphics can be pretty good at times, but there are models that looks like from a N64 game which feels out of place. The game also felt rushed as some things can be janky, like climbing ladders doesn't feel right and your hands don't have proper poses while you are climbing it, companion NPCs can be a bit dumb, but worst of all it has bugs that can get you soft locked. For instance, in a particular section that you need to carry story related items to proceed (meltabombs), I got soft locked and the game auto-saved so I had to restart the game from the start. For a game to soft lock you that easily probably means that it was not properly tested.

However, If you can disregard these flaws, then the game is actually fun. The gunplay is decent, headshotting enemies and seeing their heads rolling or shooting an arm off an enemy feels impactful, and the game passes a good sense of scale with big environments and characters that you may encounter. Overall it's a decent game if you can disregard its big issues.

This is not 40K￼ on ￼your flatscreen. This is 40K in VR! It’s night and day. So forget 40K that you’ve played on your PC or console. This game has been made specifically for an immersive experience right inside of your headset. ￼
I enjoy this game for several reasons. Although hitting a target, especially a moving target, can be a challenge. Unlike other action/shooter type games, this one does not always have a wall or pillar that you can hide behind. Sometimes you’re forced to physically crouch behind rubble or containers to avoid getting hit￼. I was pleased to find out that you can also double fist the weapons you are given, shooting from the right and left hand. L

As you defeat enemies you energize a power or powers called “faith push“. Which is a bit like using the force in a Star Wars game. 
It is a dark-ish game, but not in a scary way.￼￼ The visuals were beyond my expectations for standalone Q2! And now it’s been updated for the Q3 and Q3S! Seriously, I was impressed with the graphics. Now that the Q3 and Q3S are available, it pushes the envelope further. ￼The audio is also impressive. I ￼use the logitech pro G oculus ready headphones and they’re terrific! But playing with just Q2, Q3 and Q3S speakers are also a solid experience. This is one of the rare instances where in some ways the Q2, Q3 and Q3S sounds are more than fair, just because of the ￼environments in game that emphasize sounds such as echoes, weapons’ fire, ￼etc. ￼￼
If you’re a fan of the action/adventure/shooter you won’t be disappointed!

I did not really enjoy this at all, the combat felt bland and damage from enemies was unbalanced from what the player can do, making you wish the friendly NPCS actually helped instead of just getting extremely close to the enemy and not even help take out the tank that's been firing at me non-stop, and the story felt corny in some parts, and I hated the fact that when the next part of the mission load's, your whole load out just resets, taking away all the weapons you had originally and mess up your load out, could probably have a better 40K experience with a VR mod on space marines 2, or in a vrchat 40K world.
really fun game idk why people rate this one star just one teeny tiny bit problem i really wanna experience piloting a imperial knight would be really cool if the devs make it where you can pilot one

Gun play is pretty fun, but the damage from enemies is awful, compounded by the slow movement. The Call of Duty-style healing disincentivizes aggressive gameplay and there’s often a lack of cover, which is counterintuitive considering how many enemies can appear at once.
At the same time, it feels like there aren’t enough enemies, as I never seem to run out of ammo, even in the boss fights, which are stupidly easy once you figure out the gimmick. In a nutshell, the difficulty somehow manages to be both incredibly easy and frustrating.
Story was pretty short, even if that’s kind of expected with VR. I still had fun, but I would probably only recommend this to other people interested in Warhammer.
Big gun go boom. Heretical head goes pop.
claims to be playable sitting or standing, but has zero sitting settings making it completely unplayable unless you're standing

Purchased this a while ago because I love Warhammer.

Remembered that I got it. Got excited. Charged up the headset. DL the game.

Wow. What a disappointment. The reload mechanics are awful. The combat is awful. The meme is true: GSW will license to anything and everything regardless of quality standards.

Voice activation for faith commands not working, falling through map after checking settings or in menu, reach mechanics not working or registering, stance of character stuck in crouch, need a sitting toggle, need holster height adjustment, need a brightness setting (so dark in areas bc of lil to no lighting cant see anything)

reviews.review_title: A big name makes its way to Meta...
reviews.review_description:
Occulus / Meta has been a have of indie games and small companies, and many have made household name games from it. 

Arizona Sunshine, Zero Caliber, Vox Machina...

It's rare to have a previously well know title, franchise, or company come to Occulus / Meta, but here it is. 

And it...is...worth it. The game does an eerily good job of projecting the mentality of Battle Sisters - fighting arm of the Ecclisiarchy and Chamber Militant of the Ordo Hereticus - and it is a ton of fun.

Side note: your ally AI is pretty terrible in the open, but performs better in hallways and rooms. Remember that in certain levels.

I was hesitant to get this game because of a lot of bad reviews. But once I got it, I did not regret it. The game is genuinely beautiful, all of the models are gorgeous and truly reflects the dark and desolate vibe of 40k. The story is fantastic, even in the static universe of 40k, I think it is able to be really powerful and all of the characters have good stories themselves. There are plenty of levels and each feel super diverse. The combat, while jank at times, is genially quite fun. All of the different weapons have different play styles and I found myself really thinking about my inventory management more than a few times. Even after completing the game, The Last Bastion mode is always fun and running that with friends is genuinely exhilarating. There are a few glitches, and the weapons can be a little difficult to put away. The final boss was super underwhelmingly easy and I beat it in less than 5 minutes on my first attempt. But overall a fantastic experience in most if not all aspects. Highly recommend.

Warhammer is easily my favourite game so when i saw this i was quick to get it.It is a shooter game based off of the Adeptus Astartes fighting against the forces of chaos.Easily a buy from me. Five stars

I absolutely love this game, me and my friends play it all the time. We love the universe but there needs to be more variety!!! How can I serve the emperor properly if im only killing word bearers?? I need to kill more daemon types!!!!! If anyone can get soul assembly in touch with me I will PAY THEM TO DO IT!!!
This game is so amazing. It feels like you’re actually are a sister of battle. This is so much fun. ￼

Honestly, I really like the game. I enjoy it a lot, although I can't really seem to get the Discord to work, so I'm using my review as some questions I have for it. It seems like updates have made the game a lot better than it has in the past, so that's good. I really like it.
My question is, is there going to be any other Warhammer 40K games for the meta quest? It would be cool to get some kind of space marine game or a chaos game as well, or maybe a sequel to Battle Sister. All in all, this is a really good game.
Not nearly as janky or broken as the comments suggests. Fair priced as well.

Many bugs so much bugs but I managed to play through most of it until i got to level 7
After that i tired to go to the next level but it kept on crashing, although the bugs crush the experience somehow even without the bugs the game would still be horrible, the story has potential but the butcher it, not to the mention how buff the enemy’s are they never miss, perfect shot so annoying it’s impossible to do  ￼no hir in this game. The game mechanics are weird to. I have to speak to use a forgetable feture like slowing time. You speak but most of the time it cuts off after a split second so you cannot sat anything. That sums up my rant
Fabulous game to just play out of the blue no need to know the backstory really just start the game and play. It’s quite enjoyable experience. ￼
Not great

reviews.review_title: In the Name of the God Emperor!
reviews.review_description: So good story mode, overall a grand game, the only thing I would like to see is more updates to make it Be more expansive especially since the lore of Warhammer 40K is pretty large! I would like to see Orks, Slaanesh, Tzeentch, Nurgle and Khorne. I would love to see other chapters make a appearance such as the Crimson Fists, Space Wolves, and even the Salamanders! Bring out more with the Necrons, as well as the Heretics.

reviews.review_title: Why didn’t they put these in
reviews.review_description:
It’s a great game don’t get me wrong but I got a couple issues with it, I didn’t see a difficulty setting, no manual saves, and barely any ammo

Each issue would be fixed for me if even one of them it modified, if I could manually save I could die in one area over and over retrying without going through seven area just to get back to my og place, if there was more ammo difficulty and no manual saves would be barely an issue, and if I could lower or raise the difficulty ammo and saving would be the least of my worry’s 

But all three are huge issues for me cause my play space is small and my internet is slow so I die to something that didn’t register, or I die cause I didn’t have enough room to move either Irl or In-game

Other then these the game is great, good story, gun play, and enmities just ease fix at least one of these issues for people who don’t play vr all that often like myself cause we are usually not the best

Unless this is supposed to be like a soulslike then nevermind your decision make sense

The story mode for this game would be so much more fun if it had co-op so you can play with a friend as yes the horde mode is fun but being stuck in one area can get boring instead of being able to do the campaign with your friend

reviews.review_title: 10 minutes before a show stopping bug
reviews.review_description:
It's impossible to complete the mandatory tutorial. It says you need to collect the ammo from a crate and won't let you progress even after you have done so. If you reload a checkpoint you can progress, except it gets rid of your only weapon.

That is the entire game.

reviews.review_title: The franchise needs a new boss
reviews.review_description: I want to like every Warhammer game I try, thinking one will hit but none never do. Sadly they aren’t worth your time.

I was looking forward to playing this co-op with my husband. Having never played warhammer before didn't know the controls, so didn't have a clue how to play. Disappointed, there is no tutorial. The movement is also shockingly rubbish. Can't run, and turning is too slow to keep up with the game play. Disappointed to say the least.

It looks nice, but the interface is so bad it is close to unplayable. I spent my time trying to grab ammo - no, not the other bolter, the ammo - is it loaded? I need to look down for it to load? Wait, what is the tool-tip hovering over my head? I can't look up because it just goes up as well. Still can't reload. Okay, pause chaos! I need to reload, time out! Pick up the ammo? What ammo? I can't see anything!

Get ready for alot of that. Looks nice though.

reviews.review_title: We genuinely need more 40k be titles

reviews.review_description: Campaign is fun and last bastion is promising so far. Weapons are really satisfying and fun, overall just a really good warhammer game 👍

This game is a dream come true as a Warhammer 40K fan. Truly one of the best vr experiences I’ve ever had.    They nailed the world and the details on the guns/armors. Shame on me for not playing this sooner. THE EMPEROR PROTECTS!
Tbh repetitive and I glitch out of the map every two seconds
This game is a masterpiece every single bit I love it I even love Warhammer. I love it so much is the best masterpiece blood for the blood. God this game is.
If you are a big lorehead, you will notice many discrepancies, but if you look past that this is a amazing experience and worth the money.
Lovely game . would love to give it five stars 🌟  please devs make the controls less juddering  other than that Brilliant .and more   mosters
Literally won’t start for me I’ve payed for it, and it just loads. Up a black screen??

reviews.review_title: THIS GAME DOESN'T BACK UP TO THE CLOUD

reviews.review_description:
I was tempted to buy this at full price but then it landed on the meta+ subscription. 
I thought it was amazing, I really enjoyed the story, plot twist, and environment. The game can be a challenge in a couple of places, but it just adds to the sense of achievement. It's not the longest to play through, which works for me as other games can feel a little drawn out. 
I wouldn't have been dissatisfied if I had paid full price for it.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this review!

reviews.review_description: While the enemies of the emperor still draw breath, there can be no peace.  My faith is my shield. Beware the alien, the mutant, the heretic. An open mind is like a fortress with its gates unbarred and unguarded.

developer_response.body: FOR THE EMPEROR!

reviews.review_description: I recently bought this game and it’s OK for a meta quest game. But i want better graphics im on a quest 2 and maybe thats the reason but please update the graphics i dont want to have to return this game 😭

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your feedback with us!

reviews.review_description: It was the first game I played on meta3, and I have  to say what a game. I absolutely loved it. Would 100% recommend this game. I really hope there will be a sequel 🙏

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for sharing this positive review!

reviews.review_description: I grabbed this one from Quest+ not really expecting much, but I ended up really loving this game. The multiplayer is basically a horde mode, but the single player campaign is fantastic, and the voice acting is surprisingly good. Would love to see a sequel or a DLC with a full campaign  co-op mode. Either way I definitely would like to see more of this game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much Dave!

reviews.review_description:
Well. I went in with low expectations, as would anyone with any history in the back catalogue of 40k video games, and this blew my face off. 

Great story, great action, the visuals are on point, the lore and aesthetics of the setting are perfect. The gameplay was brilliant, the vr physics are at times a little odd, but nothing worse than any other vr experience, it's the nature of the medium. 

For any fan of Warhammer 40k, this is a must purchase. Literally annihilating heretics with bolter and chainsword. Or flamer and chainsword. Or chainsword and chainsword. 

Dual wield melta guns.

Get stuck in. For the emperor.

I really hope they make another one.

developer_response.body:
Hi Fin! Thank you so so much for this amazing review! It means a lot to us. 

For the Emperor!

reviews.review_description: Based

developer_response.body: Based

reviews.review_description: It's been a few years since this came out so it feels a little dated, but it's still an incredibly fun shooter with a lot of cool mechanics that are unique to this setting. It is so cool to shout out. Something like the emperor protects to activate powers in the middle of a game while you're running and shooting to get a moment of invincibility. I would love more games in the 40K universe, but I really thought that this perspective of an adepta sororitas battle sister was interesting and unique. Usually you play as a big hulking space Marine, and that's cool too, but to be a little bit less invulnerable unstoppable creates more interesting challenges in danger. Danger. Really great acting and great writing for the setting. I loved it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this awesome review! 

For the Emperor!

reviews.review_description: Nothing groundbreaking and the graphics are a bit outdated now (but still not looking bad, I especially liked weapon models), but there is something awesome in being able to shoot bolt guns and use chainswords. And slicing enemies with a sword in one hand and shooting them with a bolt pistol in another, made me feel like a real bada**

developer_response.body: Hey, thank you so much for leaving your feedback! We appreciate it!

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to like it
reviews.review_description: Just what any Warhammer fan would want, to be thrown into the world and experience a story first hand. The premise is there, but the gameplay is awful. I would even say that the low poly graphics on most things still fits the expectations and looks pretty decent. But, the animations, the AI, the feel of the shooting, the sound effects, all so generic and clunky that it just makes it bad. Like my title states, I really wanted to like it, but I don't think anyone that has given this 5 stars or helped with QA has any clue what it is like to play any other game. Even for just being the linear story it is, it should not feel that bad to play.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you for sharing your feedback with us! We appreciate hearing our players honest opinions, so we can utilise that information for our future projects!

reviews.review_description: First of all.. bolters are cool, but in this game it feels like shooting with a empty packed of cereals. It is horrible to aim with, and no "laser pointers".. it kinda ruined it for me..

reviews.review_title: Great game ruined by game-breaking bugs
reviews.review_description:
Just don't buy this. This game has a lot of bugs that makes it unplayable. I'm stuck in an area where I can't go anywhere as the doors are bugged.

Avoid this game at all cost.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

We are constantly working on the game, we would really appreciate more information on this bug you encountered.  Please join our Battle Sister Discord and submit a bug report - https://discord.gg/rDxchdfSpS

reviews.review_description:
If you're a 40K fan like me this game slaps hard. It makes you feel like you're a miniature on the tabletop, and wielding a bolter has long been a fantasy of mine. Love it. Great fun. As a 6'3" man, I think the Chaos Marines are a bit small but hey, that's a minor complaint.

Also now I know how a meltagun is loaded :P

developer_response.body:
Hey! 

Thank you for the amazing review, glad to hear you enjoyed wielding the bolter and killing all the heretics!

reviews.review_description:
All things considered I had a pretty great time with this one and still boot it up to see the new updates. Even if you aren’t a big Warhammer fan don’t worry, this game makes a good effort to include you in the world, story, and does a lot to help you understand your purpose. You’re a significant character with their own motivation and goals, you care about your teammates, sure, but there’s an overarching goal to achieve within the story. This game reminded me of a Call of Duty campaign, while it’s pretty linear it was a cohesive and action packed experience throughout. There’s a bit of exploration for ammo and lore bits but otherwise it’s a straightforward experience. It makes a good effort to mix things up with objectives and set pieces but the shooting and story is the main appeal here. 

This truly is a Warhammer fans dream come true. Fighting along an Astartes and Battle Sister using iconic weapons like the Bolters and Lazgun, chain swords and even a few cool powers were all here and felt excellent. There’s  a solid arsenal of powerful weapons to use, although the melee weapons were finicky. The enemy variants were solid and fun to fight.  They even included a couple boss fights and while it’s kinda goofy looking they were well done. I enjoyed the characters and while I think the game was too short the voice acting and story was solid. The animations were good and the AI teammates usually fought better than I did. You miss Julius and his infinite ammo. That first time meeting an Astartes was awesome. He truly towers over you. The sense of scale with this game in general was fantastic, plenty of cool enemies with great designs. Not to mention the great audio design. The world looked great for the quest 2. They did a fantastic job overall. 

Like I said you don’t need to be a warhammer fan to enjoy this game it’ll just help you understand the overall vibe a lot more. I’d be quick to recommend this game to anyone in need of a fun shooter, it’s fast paced, it’s engaging, has plenty of stuff to shoot and I think even new VR players would find this game easy to pick up and enjoy.

reviews.review_title: I really wanted to get into this
reviews.review_description:
I love almost everything about this game, truly my only complaint is the lack of an option to adjust where the ammo pouch is.
I play sitting down and cannot reach the ammo pouch to reload or grab weapon holsters without having to turn my entire body and reach below my chair.
These issues make the game unplayable (for me) without standing. 
Is it possible to please add an option to adjust the height/width/location of the ammo pouch/holsters?
I understand we are playing a female but I am nowhere near as thin as the character we are playing.
2 stars for now because I am now stuck with a game I literally cannot physically play yet, if this feature is added I will happily change it to 5 stars.

developer_response.body:
Dear TrulyGrim,
Sorry to hear your having difficulty with the Ammo Pouch, we have discussed adding a system that will let the User adjust the Ammo Pouch and Holster.
I'll put a ticket in for it and hopefully it can be addressed in a future.

Please join us on the Official Discord: https://discord.gg/kUFpBKqaGB

reviews.review_title: I’m too fat to grab grenades
reviews.review_description:
I’m a huge warhammer fanboy, first off. Saw this in the store and no question. Had to buy it. As any other WH fan knows, they don’t have the greatest track record for gaming. Half of them are terrible. Well this one did not disappoint! 
  I’m a picky gamer and don’t like playing as women. Don’t care in this game. (My avatars always look like me as well) 
  The controls are simple to get the hang of, gun play feels satisfying and I got a halfchub when I found my first chainsword. This game is hard but not dark souls hard. There will be a few spots you’ll die and have to retry a few times but it’s nothing game ending aggravating.
  Now, the headline, right. Hahaha I’m too fat to grab grenades. You store grenades on the front of your belt in this game. Well when you look down at yourself, you’re a skinny, leather clad battle sister. I’m a beer bellied 42 year old man. In order to get the highlight on the grenade for me to grab it, I have to shove my hand under my gut and into my abdomen in order to get to where the grenade is stored on my skinny female VR self 😂😂 a little frustrating in the heat of battle but more funny than anything else. 
  Anyways, if you’re a WH person, you’ll love it. Even if you aren’t it’s a very solid solo FPS shooter with a good solo campaign if you’re like me and not into the whole “being around other people” thing, even in VR.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that you had issue grabbing the grenades.
Please feel free to join us on the discord and we can try to make the game more accessible : https://discord.gg/kUFpBKqaGB

reviews.review_description: I quit the game after 5 minutes. Graphics look like PS2 and sounds are even worse. At Oculus, please do not quit PC VR this game is a good example why you should not do so! UPDATE: Since the release I bought it two times and retruned it. I really tried to like this game. With the delivered updates I thought it would be better but unfurtunately it is not. Now I bought it in Sale with update 1.1.13 and what should I say, it is just disapointing. This game is not polished everthing feels odd, no immersion. So I will return the game a third time and will not try it again. It is no good game. Update: WTF I can't return this crap game because the 3 times I purchsed it I played to long in summary.... Now this waste of time and money is bound to my account. Dont buy it, honestly it is crap.

developer_response.body:
Dear Draven, were sorry that you've had a negative experience with the game.
We have performed a few different updates over time to improve the graphics and sound of the game.
If you can I recommend playing the RIFT version of the game through Oculus Link on your Quest and using headphones for better sound quality.

If you have any further feedback please contact us on the Official Discord:  https://discord.gg/kUFpBKqaGB

reviews.review_description: If there is anything that is really annoying & unfair it is when you buy a game & cant complete as is too hard. I have been stuck for days battling a level & now give up. I bought the game, why cant there be an easy mode switch or cheat so I may play the game I paid for. I give up.

developer_response.body:
Dear CountB,

Sorry to hear that you've been having difficulty getting through the game.
We are planning on adding difficulty levels to the game's campaign in a future update.

In the meantime I would recommend taking full advantage of your Faith abilities as they can really help turn the tide of a battle, or make good use of cover / sidestepping enemy fire.

We have a Discord for the game where our admins and other players will be more than happy to help you. https://discord.gg/9WR6Rr8DSs

reviews.review_description: In the first level I became stuck between a dirt embankment and some metal or wood supports and then the very last guy wouldn’t die no matter how many times I shot him.  The last guy that was on the cliff to the right, I shot him with probably 300 bullets and there’s no way I could’ve missed him yet he wouldn’t die so I couldn’t go to the second level.  Totally pointless.

developer_response.body:
Hey Brilliant,

Our latest update has been released which includes a huge graphical overhaul for Quest 2 with increased texture resolution, dynamic shadows and lighting improvements across all levels. This updated also added new achievements, improved audio clarity and fixed a lot of outstanding issues called out in your review. You can see our full release notes via the blog on our Oculus page! We're not stopping here though, our next update will include our Last Bastion Replayable Horde mode.

reviews.review_description: I got scammed out of my money don’t buy this game  I spent all my birthday money on it.😭😭😭😭

reviews.review_description: I like this game a lot its prolly the best quest ve game I have played so far aside from drunken bar fight
